repo_model.summary: Kapso is a RelBench starter repository for generic-search experiments. The root candidate loads a sanitized task cache, produces task-family-specific validation and test predictions, and relies on an immutable grader to score official validation metrics.

entrypoints.how_to_run: The harness invokes `python main.py --debug` for a fast contract run and `python main.py` for a full run.

where_to_edit.role: Primary candidate implementation: task dispatch, model or heuristic integration, and prediction artifact generation.
Shared RelBench loading, output saving, temporal-safety, and recommendation-metric utilities.
Recommendation ranking and co-occurrence candidate-generation algorithms.
Reusable heterogeneous temporal GNN model used by the vendored examples.
Reference entity classification/regression GNN training pipeline to adapt into main.py.
Stronger official ID-aware recommendation reference implementation.

claims.statement: The root candidate boundary reads the dataset and task identity from environment variables, loads the RelBench task with downloads disabled, determines validation/test sizes, and writes both prediction arrays under KAPSO_RUN_DATA_DIR.

evidence.description: Defines the candidate main function.
Loads dataset/task identity from RELBENCH_DATASET and RELBENCH_TASK with download=False.
Reads validation and test table lengths.
Uses KAPSO_RUN_DATA_DIR and saves validation/test prediction files.

claims.statement: The baseline dispatches by task family: recommendation tasks receive tiled ranked destination IDs, binary classification receives 0.5 probabilities, multiclass classification receives uniform class scores, and all remaining task types receive the training-target median.

evidence.description: Begins the RecommendationTask branch.
Creates constant binary predictions.
Creates uniform multiclass predictions.
Falls back to a median target prediction for other task types.

claims.statement: The evaluation layer runs main.py in an isolated subprocess, validates prediction artifacts, computes official RelBench validation metrics, archives full-fidelity runs, and emits a machine-readable manifest containing the score of record.

evidence.description: Runs the candidate from the repository root.
Starts the candidate as a separate subprocess.
Checks that both prediction files exist.
Computes task evaluation metrics on validation predictions.
Archives full-fidelity runs.
Prints the manifest with the primary validation score.

claims.statement: common.py provides the intended solution adapter: it constructs a TaskContext containing dataset, task, database, and train/val/test tables; saves predictions using the contract paths; and exposes temporal-safety and fast MAP@K helpers.

evidence.description: Defines TaskContext.
Loads the sanitized dataset/task and constructs the context.
Defines the shared prediction-saving function.
Defines the temporal leakage assertion.
Defines the MAP@K helper.

claims.statement: The recommendation heuristic ranks each source's historical destinations using exponentially time-decayed interaction counts, uses recent or historical global popularity as a fallback, and fills remaining slots with distinct IDs while respecting the seed-time cutoff.

evidence.description: Documents the ranked output and temporal seed behavior.
Censors interaction history at the seed time.
Builds a recent-window popularity fallback.
Computes exponential time-decay weights.
Combines per-source history with popularity candidates.
Uses a distinct-ID pathological fallback.

claims.statement: The vendored examples cover official RelBench reference families including entity GNNs, ID-GNN and two-tower recommendation models, autocomplete GNNs, LightGBM baselines, and trivial entity baselines.

evidence.description: Maps the vendored entity GNN implementation.
Maps ID-GNN and two-tower recommendation examples.
Maps the autocomplete GNN.
Maps LightGBM baselines.
Maps the trivial entity baseline.

claims.statement: Every run, including debug mode, must write val_predictions.npy and test_predictions.npy with rows positionally aligned to the corresponding RelBench validation and test tables; validation predictions must come from a model that did not train on validation labels.

evidence.description: Requires both prediction files after every run and positional row alignment.
Requires validation predictions to be out-of-sample.
Shared saver writes both required files.

claims.statement: Prediction formats are task-specific: binary outputs are float probabilities in [0,1] with shape (N,), regression outputs are float (N,), multiclass outputs are float (N,C), and recommendation outputs are integer ranked distinct destination IDs with shape (N,K) and IDs in [0,num_dst).

evidence.description: Defines the required prediction shapes, dtypes, ranges, and recommendation ranking constraints.
Validates recommendation shape and integer dtype.
Checks recommendation destination ID bounds and duplicate warnings.
Determines multiclass expected shape.
Validates binary probability range.

claims.statement: All RelBench data access must use download=False against the sanitized read-only cache; test labels are physically absent and must not be probed.

evidence.description: Requires download=False and forbids probing for test labels.
Documents sanitized-cache loading without downloads.
Calls get_dataset with download=False.
Calls get_task with download=False.

claims.statement: The search score is the official validation primary metric; the complete validation split is always scored, while fraction and seed are only echoed in the manifest and must not reduce the scored item set.

evidence.description: States that fast and full fidelity score the complete validation split.
Says fraction and seed are manifest metadata only.
Defines the manifest score as the official validation primary metric.
Computes validation metrics.
Places the primary validation metric in the manifest score.

claims.statement: Test predictions are collected as artifacts but cannot be scored inside the loop because the sanitized cache contains no test labels; final test evaluation occurs outside the search loop.

evidence.description: States that test predictions are collected but cannot be scored in the grader.
Places final test evaluation outside the loop.
Archives test predictions while leaving private test metrics empty.

claims.statement: The required sanity check is to run both `python main.py --debug` and `python main.py`; debug must finish quickly while still producing both prediction files.

evidence.description: States that the harness runs the program twice.
Specifies the debug command.
Specifies the full command.
Requires artifacts after every run, including debug.

claims.statement: After writing predictions, run `python kapso_datasets/check_predictions.py` to validate files, shapes, finite values, binary ranges, and recommendation ID constraints before invoking the harness.

evidence.description: Recommends the local prediction checker.
Checks both validation and test splits.
Loads prediction arrays without pickle.
Validates non-recommendation shapes.
Prints successful split checks.

claims.statement: A grader dry run can be performed with `python kapso_evaluation/grader.py --fidelity fast`; success includes official validation metrics and a KAPSO_EVAL_MANIFEST line containing the score.

evidence.description: Recommends verifying the suite with a fast-fidelity dry run.
Defines the fast/full fidelity CLI.
Prints official validation metrics.
Prints the machine-readable manifest score.

claims.statement: In the current root baseline, --debug is only reported in the final log line; it does not select a cheaper training or inference path, so replacing the baseline with real training must explicitly implement debug subsampling or reduced epochs.

evidence.description: The only main execution path is defined here.
Uses the debug flag only in the printed diagnostic.
Requires debug mode to finish fast.
Requires aggressive debug subsampling and a short build for adapted examples.

claims.statement: The fallback branch is broad: any task that is not a RecommendationTask, binary classification, or multiclass classification is treated as a median-target prediction, with no explicit regression-type check.

evidence.description: Handles binary classification.
Handles multiclass classification.
Routes every remaining task type to the fallback.
Computes the training target median.

claims.statement: The recommendation baseline assumes eval_k does not exceed the destination-ID space: it emits arange(k) without checking num_dst, while the contract requires every ID to be below num_dst.

evidence.description: Reads task.eval_k.
Creates recommendation IDs with arange(k).
Requires recommendation IDs to lie in [0,num_dst).

claims.statement: Direct manual execution of the root baseline requires KAPSO_RUN_DATA_DIR to be set because main.py indexes that environment variable directly, even though the shared helper offers a local default directory.

evidence.description: Directly requires KAPSO_RUN_DATA_DIR.
Provides a default ./kapso_output directory in the helper layer.

claims.statement: The root candidate depends on RelBench and NumPy; the evaluation suite documents that no setup beyond Python with relbench and numpy is needed for the baseline grader path.

evidence.description: Imports NumPy.
Imports RelBench task classes and task loader.
Documents the Python, relbench, and numpy runtime requirement.

claims.statement: The vendored neural and feature-model examples add heavier optional dependencies including PyTorch, PyTorch Geometric, torch-frame, tqdm, and sentence-transformers for text embeddings.

evidence.description: Imports PyTorch.
Imports PyTorch Geometric data structures.
Imports torch-frame statistics.
Imports tqdm and graph neighbor-loading components.
Imports sentence-transformers.

claims.statement: Runtime configuration is environment-driven: the harness supplies dataset/task identity, primary metric, cache and work directories, timeouts, and the per-run output directory; shared candidate caches use KAPSO_SHARED_CACHE_DIR when configured.

evidence.description: Lists dataset/task, metric, work directory, timeouts, and cache environment variables.
Lists KAPSO_SHARED_CACHE_DIR.
Defines the shared cache directory environment behavior.
Creates the shared cache directory.

book.summary: Kapso is a RelBench starter repository for generic-search experiments. The root candidate loads a sanitized task cache, produces task-family-specific validation and test predictions, and relies on an immutable grader to score official validation metrics.

toc.one_liner: RelBench runtime, NumPy, optional reference-model stack, and harness environment
